goals.short_name: Schools, hospitals and campuses are built for B.C.'s growing communities

main_services.name: Find out about capital projects in your community
Access government real estate and facilities services

goals.short_name: Major infrastructure projects are delivered faster and more efficiently

main_services.name: Get faster approvals for a provincially significant infrastructure project
Partner with government on complex capital project procurement

goals.short_name: Construction workers and businesses are paid fairly and safely employed

main_services.name: Get paid on time on a construction project
Work on a major public construction project

acts.summary: Formalizes the powers of the Ministry of Infrastructure and creates up to six tools — priority permitting reviews, qualified professional certifications, expedited environmental assessment, automatic low-risk permits, local government planning flexibility, and faster agreement frameworks — to speed up review and delivery of projects designated as provincially significant, without reducing environmental, safety or Indigenous consultation standards.

sections.title: Priority permitting reviews and qualified professional certifications
sections.description: Designated projects go to the front of the line for permit reviews, and over time qualified professionals may be allowed to certify compliance with provincial standards.

services.name: Request priority permitting review for a designated infrastructure project
services.ms_name: Get faster approvals for a provincially significant infrastructure project

sections.title: Local government planning flexibility and agreements
sections.description: Local governments can ask the province to temporarily waive provincial land use planning rules (such as official community plan updates) for designated projects, and use a framework to reach faster agreements with proponents.

services.name: Request local government planning flexibility for a designated project
services.ms_name: Get faster approvals for a provincially significant infrastructure project

acts.citation: Bill 20, 2025 (43rd Parliament, 1st Session) — Royal Assent November 27, 2025; not yet in force
acts.summary: Sets mandatory payment deadlines and a fast-track adjudication process for payment disputes between owners, contractors and subcontractors on construction projects. Responsibility for the Act moved from the Ministry of Attorney General to the Ministry of Infrastructure; regulations and an adjudication authority are being developed before the Act comes into force.

sections.title: Proper invoices and payment deadlines
sections.description: Contractors deliver a 'proper invoice'; owners must pay in full within 28 days or notify of non/partial payment within 14 days; contractors must then pay subcontractors within 7 days of receiving payment.

services.name: Get paid on time as a contractor or subcontractor
services.ms_name: Get paid on time on a construction project

sections.title: Adjudication of payment disputes
sections.description: Payment-related disputes can be referred to an adjudicator drawn from a registry of qualified professionals, with determinations issued within weeks and enforcement backed by interest charges or work suspension.

services.name: Resolve a construction payment dispute through adjudication
services.ms_name: Get paid on time on a construction project
